steps.title: Find the vertically opposite angle at m
steps.content: The labeled 111° lies below line $m$ on the left side of transversal $k$. Its vertically opposite angle (above line $m$, right side) is also 111°.

steps.title: Apply corresponding angles property
steps.content: The angle $x°$ at line $l$ (above the line, right side of $k$) and the angle at line $m$ (above the line, right side of $k$) are in the same relative position at their respective intersections. When a transversal crosses parallel lines, such corresponding angles are equal: $$x = 111$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
